<!-- Generated by the darkmode-chroma-proposal-generator workflow (scripts/darkmode-chroma-proposal-generator.workflow.js), 2026-06-24. A STARTING spec to calibrate tomorrow, not final. -->

# Dark-Mode Chroma Reduction — Starting Spec (v1)

A single decision-ready proposal to tweak. Default is identity, so nothing changes until a caller opts in.

## 1. Recommended starting point

**Hybrid of Proposal C (`reduce(L,H)`) and Proposal B (the L-taper), shipped with hue ON but conservative.**

The three proposals are the same wiring with progressively more shape:
- **A** is hue-blind, L-stepped, fills exempt.
- **B** adds a real L-taper (parabola peaking in the fill band), fills trimmed.
- **C** adds the hue term that the research says is the load-bearing axis (blue glows, the gamut won't clamp it; yellow/green self-clamp).

The research is unambiguous that **a flat cut is wrong** — it under-treats blue and over-treats yellow/green. So C's `reduce(L,H) = retain(L) · hueFactor(H)` is the right *shape*. But C's headline cost is calibration (most knobs). We tame that by starting from C's structure with **gentle depths** and an **explicit fill-exemption switch** so the owner can dial doctrine vs. perceptual cut in one place.

The one substantive correction to all three: the research's "L-dependence shape" finding is that the engine's existing `chromaMultiplier` **already** rises with dark L (0.30→0.84) — deep surfaces are already quietest. So `retain(L)` should **NOT** pull hardest at the darkest stops (C and A both do, which double-cuts an already-tiny chroma). Start `retain(L)` near 1.0 at the bottom and concentrate the cut in the **hot fill/boosted-signal mid-band (L 0.55–0.80)**, which is B's insight. Final recommendation merges **B's L-curve** with **C's hue term**.

### `reduce(L, H) = clamp(retain(L) · hueFactor(H), MIN_RETAIN, 1.0)`

**`retain(L)`** — concentrate the cut on the hot mid-band; leave the already-quiet deep surfaces and the gamut-clamped text tier alone:

| dark L | retain(L) | what lives here |
|---|---|---|
| ≤ 0.20 | 1.00 | deep app-bg (already 30% brand C via chromaMult — don't double-cut) |
| 0.28 | 0.97 | subtle stop ~4 |
| 0.40 | 0.90 | subtle border tier |
| 0.55 | 0.82 | hot accent / low fills |
| 0.64 | 0.78 | **peak cut** — boosted signal surfaces + vivid fills |
| 0.72 | 0.82 | brand fill min-L (ease back; fills want identity) |
| 0.80 | 0.92 | accent text (gamut envelope narrowing) |
| ≥ 0.90 | 1.00 | body-tint text (gamut already clamps to near-ink) |

Closed form (single tuning knob `DEPTH`): `retain(L) = clamp(1 − DEPTH · max(0, 1 − ((L−0.64)/0.46)²), MIN_RETAIN, 1.0)`, **start DEPTH = 0.22** (gentle; 0.30 = recommended-aggressive, 0.40 = max).

**`hueFactor(H)`** — cosine bump peaking in blue, floored ≥0.99 across green/yellow so the gamut leads there:

| hue band (OKLCH °) | hueFactor | why |
|---|---|---|
| blue/indigo 240–290 (peak 265) | 0.82 | chromostereopsis glow; gamut won't clamp dark blue → manual cut essential |
| violet/magenta 290–330 | 0.88 | short-wave glow |
| red 10–40 | 0.92 | red bloom/advance |
| cyan 190–220 | 0.93 | reads electric on dark |
| green 140–170 | 0.99 | near a B-B invariant; cusp self-clamps |
| yellow 90–110 | 1.00 | high-L cusp already forces desaturation |

Closed form: `hueFactor(H) = 1 − 0.18 · max(0, cos(rad(H − 265)))^1.5`, then floor to ≥0.99 for H ∈ [120, 175]. **Start depth 0.18** (softer than C's 0.22 — easier to dial up than to walk back a too-aggressive blue).

`MIN_RETAIN = 0.50` — hard floor so no composite ever muds a fill into achromatic gray (research: on dark surfaces chroma is doing double duty as the discriminability signal; never strip it fully).

Worked composites (vivid brand, brandC ≈ 0.13): blue fill stop 9 (L 0.70) → 0.82·0.82 ≈ **0.67×**; green fill → 0.82·0.99 ≈ **0.81×**; blue subtle stop 1 (L 0.18) → 1.00·0.82 ≈ **0.82×**; any text stop 12 → gamut clamp dominates regardless.

## 2. Where it plugs in (grounded) + composition without double-counting

**Verified against `/Users/emilygerrity/okchroma/src/engine/colorEngine.ts` (line numbers confirmed by reading the file).**

The `chromaCurve` seam (L374–375) **cannot** carry this: its signature is `(L, mode) => C` — it *replaces* chroma, has no input C to scale, no H, and is mode-shared with the light/neutral generator. Confirmed at L375: `opts?.chromaCurve ? opts.chromaCurve(L, mode) : nativeC`.

**Add a new dark-only option** `darkChromaReduce?: (L: number, H: number) => number` on `GenerateOptions`, default `() => 1` ⇒ byte-identical. Introduce one wrapper next to `cAt` (after L375):

```ts
const darkCAt = (L, H, C, floorMin = 0) =>
  Math.max(floorMin, cAt('dark', L, C) * (opts?.darkChromaReduce?.(L, H) ?? 1))
```

Swap `cAt('dark', …)` → `darkCAt(…)` at exactly these confirmed dark render sites:
- **Subtle stops 1–8** — L581 (pass the torsioned `H`; `floorMin` = the `applyChromaFloor` value for that stop so a <1 factor can't sink it below `DARK_FLOOR`).
- **Fill 9/10** — L584–585, and the enforce-contrast rebuild at L605–606 (`floorMin = 0`). **Gated by the fill-exemption switch** (see knob below).
- **Text 11/12** — L588, L591 (`floorMin` = their floor).
- **Highlight rung 13/14** — L659 `darkHlC`. Recommend **leaving exempt** initially (it is already darkened-to-hold-white identity machinery).

Leave **every** `cAt('light', …)` site untouched (L403/511/538-539/556/628) — light mode and the neutral generator are unchanged.

**Composition (no double-count):**
- Runs **after** `applyChromaFloor` (L577/587/590) and **after** `cAt`/`chromaCurve` — so the floor still sets the minimum and neutral's curve still composes; `reduce()` is a clean terminal multiply.
- **Do NOT route through `subtleChromaScale`/`subtleC`.** Confirmed at L370: `subtleC = brandC * subtleChromaScale`, and signals set `subtleChromaScale = subtleChromaBoost` (>1) affecting **only stops 1–8**. Applying the reduction at `darkCAt` (post-floor, per-site) multiplies the already-boosted subtle chroma **once**, the same factor for brands and signals — orthogonal to the boost by construction. Routing it back through `subtleC` would compound with the signal boost asymmetrically (the bug to avoid).
- **Collider** muted fill (L572, `DARK_COLLIDER_MUTED_CHROMA_SCALE = 0.55`) already does its own scale; let it compose (0.55 · retain ≈ 0.46 — slightly dustier rose) or exempt it. Owner's call; default = compose (colliders are the one place over-reduction is wanted).
- **`darkRefY` (L498–500) left UNSCALED** — it solves each stop's L against pre-reduction chroma, making post-reduction L very slightly conservative on luminance. Within the "don't touch lightness" constraint; flag only if chasing exact-Y later.

## 3. What changes visibly / what is deliberately left alone

**Changes:**
- **Subtle surfaces (1–8):** blue/violet app-bg tints stop vibrating/glowing against near-black; yellow/green ladders look essentially unchanged (already gamut-desaturated there).
- **Signal surfaces:** boosted signal subtle/alert backgrounds (L ≈ 0.55–0.63) stop buzzing — the taper self-concentrates here because they enter with `subtleChromaBoost` already multiplied in, so the same factor trims more absolute chroma off them.
- **Fills (9/10):** blue/violet fills get a deliberate ~22–33% trim (read as calm brand color, not a haloing electric block); green/yellow/orange fills stay vivid. **Only if the fill-exemption switch is OFF** (see knob 4).
- **Text (11/12):** near-invisible change — the gamut envelope near white (`maxChromaAt(0.94) ≈ 0.02–0.05`) already dominates.

**Deliberately left alone:**
- **Lightness — never touched.** This is a chroma-only multiply; L stays exactly where the engine solved it. (The chroma⇄lightness "relief valve" the research describes is explicitly out of scope for v1.)
- **Light mode + neutral generator** — byte-identical (no `cAt('light')` site changes).
- **Every currently-blessed render** — byte-identical until a brand/theme opts into `darkChromaReduce`.

## 4. Open questions / knobs to calibrate tomorrow

1. **`DEPTH` (L-taper) and hue depth** — start 0.22 / 0.18. Sweep the brand fleet; dial up only if dark mode still reads electric. Easier to deepen than to walk back.
2. **Fill exemption — the doctrine decision.** Engine doctrine (stopTable, "fills keep identity / never reduce fill chroma — pulling fill chroma down manufactured mustard golds") says exempt fills. The research says blue/violet fills are the #1 perceptual offender *because* lift-only leaves them glowing. **Make it a one-line switch:** `retain = 1.0 for L ≥ 0.63` (exempt) vs. trim. Per the project's "explain before engine changes" / "no corrective patch layers" memory, this deviation **must be explicitly approved** before it ships. Recommended starting posture: **trim blue/violet fills only** (hueFactor does the gating), exempt green/yellow/orange fills automatically (their hueFactor ≈ 1.0).
3. **Per-hue factors** — verify the cosine bump has no visible step where two adjacent-hue brands straddle the green/yellow ≥0.99 floor (soft kink near 120°/175°). Confirm the blue cut (0.82) isn't too aggressive on already-muted blue brands.
4. **Contrast floor so fills don't go muddy** — confirm `MIN_RETAIN = 0.50` plus the per-stop `floorMin` keeps every meaningful fill above **APCA |Lc| ≥ 15** vs. its surface (the research's fill-discernibility floor; below it = invisible). Eyeball one low-chroma brand (C ≈ 0.03) to confirm `DARK_FLOOR` still wins.
5. **Signal sanity check** — effective signal-subtle cut is `subtleChromaBoost · retain`. Verify on one signal (e.g. red) that alerts don't drop below their alert register after composition.
6. **Off-white text cap (separate, optional)** — the research strongly recommends capping primary text at L ≈ 0.90–0.93 (anti-halation), never pure white. This spec does **not** touch L, so it's out of scope here — but flag whether dark stop 12's rootL already sits ≤0.93 or wants a separate cap proposal. Don't fold it into this chroma change.
7. **Abney/B-B hue-fidelity nudge** — when blue chroma is cut, perceived hue can drift purple/green vs. the light sibling. Research flags a small co-correction; **defer** (adds calibration cost, not needed for v1).

---

**TL;DR:** Start from C's `reduce(L,H)` structure but with B's L-curve (cut the hot mid-band, not the already-quiet deep stops) and gentler depths. Wire as a new dark-only `darkChromaReduce(L,H)` multiply via a `darkCAt` wrapper at the confirmed dark sites (L581/584-585/588/591/605-606), default identity, post-floor so it composes cleanly with `chromaMultiplier` and the signal `subtleChromaBoost` without double-counting. Tomorrow's two real decisions: **(a)** how deep, and **(b)** whether blue/violet fills get trimmed (needs explicit doctrine sign-off).

Grounding file: `/Users/emilygerrity/okchroma/src/engine/colorEngine.ts` (sites verified). Constants: `/Users/emilygerrity/okchroma/src/engine/stopTable.ts`.
